heroHeadline: SNA Seizes Baidoa; Laftagareen Resigns; Puntland Withdraws Recognition of Federal Government
heroSubtitle: In Somalia's gravest federal crisis since independence, SNA forces seized Baidoa on March 30 — the Southwest State capital — forcing President Laftagareen's resignation. Puntland announced it withdraws recognition of the FGS on March 31. Meanwhile, UNICEF warns 6.5 million face acute hunger, 50,000+ are newly displaced by the Baidoa clashes, and the US air campaign against al-Shabaab and ISIS-Somalia continues at record pace.
footerNote: Data sourced from ACLED, OCHA, UNSOM, AFRICOM, UN Monitoring Group, and major international outlets. Casualty figures contested where noted.
